author: doll-agent
content:
Closed as architecturally blocked by #365 (Element trait is sealed via private::Sealed in ferray-core). Adding 'impl Element for DualNumber' from inside ferray-autodiff is impossible by design — only ferray-core can implement Element for any concrete type because the marker trait is sealed.

Two paths to actually fix this, both bigger than a refactor under #186:
1. Move DualNumber into ferray-core as a primitive type (alongside Complex). ferray-autodiff would then re-export it and add the gradient/jacobian helpers on top. Removes the ferray-autodiff → ferray-core dependency direction conflict.
2. Add an opt-in unsealing mechanism (e.g. an extension trait) in #365.

The standalone-DualNumber-in-Array<T, D> use case the issue describes really does need the trait unsealing (#365) or the type-relocation (option 1 above). Tracking that as the umbrella; not actionable independently.

In the meantime, the Python binding for DualNumber (ferray.autodiff.DualNumber, landed in ferray-python phase 4) covers the user-facing 'dual numbers in arrays' need by stacking individual DualNumber pyclass instances; the Rust-side ergonomics issue stays parked on #365.
